ELEMENT #4: THE ACTION THRESHOLD
ELEMENT #5: THE PAIN THRESHOLD
FAST TRACK MODULE 3:
THE INNER GAME OF SALES
In the second module of the Straight Line Fast Track system you learned the true meaning of rapport
and the critical elements of influence needed to close any sale.
Let’s review…
For every successful sale, your prospect must have absolute certainty in each of the first three
Elements of Influence:
The Three 10s
10 	10	10
ELEMENT #1
FIRST 10
They love
your product
ELEMENT #2
SECOND 10
They trust and
connect with you.
ELEMENT #3
THIRD 10
They trust and
connect with
your company.
Remember, the final two elements are inversely proportionate and can affect your ability to produce
certainty in the first three elements of influence.
The first two modules have taught you some of the key fundamentals of sales success, focusing
on strategies between the interaction of the prospect and the salesperson. In this module, let’s shift
the focus to the Inner World—what goes on in your thoughts and emotions. It is here that you will
uncover your true potential for making progress and producing real results.

-- 3 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 4
THE INNER GAME OF SALES
Mastering the ability to sell is everything in life. It’s the single most important skill because in every
aspect of your life, you are either influencing others (conveying your thoughts in a way that connects
and empowers) or you are failing.
In order to master this area you must first convince yourself that you have everything it takes to
achieve massive wealth and success in life. This begins in your mind. After all, the first person you
must persuade is yourself.
TIP
Everybody has areas they need to improve upon. So, if you lack a specific skill—go out and
get it and perfect it.
4 PRINCIPLES OF THE INNER WORLD
There are four principles that when aligned become an unstoppable force for taking action.
State Management: being able to trigger the right state in the right moment—an emotional
state is how you feel in the moment.
Beliefs: the way you feel about something as a result of your experiences and emotions.
We have both empowering (move us forward) and disempowering (hold us back) beliefs.
Your lowest-level limiting belief always holds you back—this is the one that stops you from
taking action.
Standards (or Thermostat): what you tolerate in life—the “musts” you choose to live
up to in life, where you will not tolerate anything less. Your standards will dictate
long-term success.
Focus: the concentration of energy or attention. Keep laser focused on where you want to
go (the ultimate vision) versus your fears. What you focus on will determine the direction in
which you move.
1
2
3
4
KEY
Ultimate success is achieved when you master both your Inner and Outer Worlds.

-- 4 of 9 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 5
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
TIP
When you go to take action, ask yourself: What is the worst thing that could actually happen?
• 	Use this question with your prospects because it is on the mind of every person you are
trying to influence.
• 	Human beings are fear-based creatures.
• 	Show massive value, but also mitigate the risk so the close makes sense logically
and emotionally.
THE PATH OF REASON VS. THE PATH OF RESULTS
There are only two types of paths in life:
If you were truly honest with yourself, which path do you take most often?
WHAT PREVENTS US FROM ACHIEVING SUCCESS?
So, what is it that holds people back from being successful at the highest level? There are always four
universal obstacles for salespeople or entrepreneurs.
Overwhelm. People who feel overpowered emotionally get stuck in their reasons and no
action is taken.
They don’t have a powerful “why.” Most people do not understand the deeper meaning
behind “why” they want something. Without that, it becomes difficult to create the vision
needed to take action and produce results.
Not knowing how to be wrong. People learn more from their failures than their mistakes.
If you are not able to take risks along the way you will not experience the deepest level
of success.
They can’t sell their way out of a paper bag—the ability to close the deal. You must have
the ability to influence and persuade others to take action or your efforts are worthless.
4
3
2
1
People who take the path of reason
are those that will give you every
reason or excuse as to why they
cannot do something.
People who take the path of results
will make it happen regardless of any
reason. They are focused on the end
goal not the path to get there.

-- 5 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 6
STRAIGHT LINE SYSTEM: SALES AND MARKETING
In this program, we focus on the importance of marketing and persuasion—two invaluable tools in the
Straight Line Master System of Ultimate Success. The four elements of the inner game are what drive
success in each of these areas.
It is important to understand that in order to reach the highest success you must be able to influence
others and market your products or services effectively.
You could have the best marketing strategies out there, but if you can’t persuade or close the deal,
you will fail. Remember, without a close, most people will not buy!
In the absence of marketing, it is still possible to make money at sales, but it will be much more
difficult. The idea of the Straight Line System is maximized when your ability to market and sell align.
Once you are truly effective at both, you will soar to success in all aspects of your life.

your standards.

business and manage it
impeccably to grow to a
high level.

percentage of cashflow
and re-invest it.

to market your
products and
services effectively
You must know how to
influence others.

-- 6 of 9 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 7
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
ACTION PLAN
STEP 1
Let’s recall a key lesson from this module—the first person you must persuade is yourself. Using
some of the principles learned from the Inner World, let’s look at how you would progress a prospect
along the straight line.
When you first begin to establish rapport with a prospect, how do you manage your state effectively?
Are you calm and confident or are you nervous and fidgety at first? What are ways you use state
management to build rapport?
List some more empowering states you can use in the first fifteen seconds with a prospect to
build rapport.
Where is your focus at the open of a sales interaction? In what ways does it change as you progress
the prospect? Are you focusing on small things or the bigger picture?
What is your primary focus—meaning your ultimate vision for yourself?
How can using the power of focus help you with your prospect? How can you shift their focus to
maintain control of the sale?

-- 7 of 9 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 8
STEP 2: YOUR PATH TO ULTIMATE SUCCESS
As discussed, there are people who take the path of reasons and those who take the path of results.
Think of a time when you did not follow through. What were the reasons you used to justify not
following through?
What is the deeper reason you did not follow through? Chances are likely the reason involves fear. If
it does not, you may have not thought of the real reason yet. Keep digging—this could involve one of
your most disempowering beliefs.
Think of a time in your life that you felt truly proud. What is a time when you accomplished something great
and thought you were unstoppable. What was the pathway to results that made this moment possible?

-- 8 of 9 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 9
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
Now, list a compelling personal goal to accomplish using this Fast Track system.
What excuses or reasons will you not let get in the way of accomplishing this goal? (Hint: They may
be similar to what you wrote above.)
What will be your pathway to results? What results will you need to accomplish along the way to
ultimately get you to conquer your goal?

-- 9 of 9 --
